final_output.final_deliverable:
## Extracted from Wikipedia: *Python (programming language)*
Source page visited: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language)

### 1) Year Python was first released (first appeared)
- **1991**
  - Evidence (infobox): The page lists **“First appeared 20 February 1991 (1991-02-20)”**. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language) — quote: "First appeared\n20 February 1991\n(\n1991-02-20\n)")

### 2) Name of Python’s creator
- **Guido van Rossum**
  - Evidence (infobox): The page states **“Designed by Guido van Rossum”**. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language) — quote: "Designed by\nGuido van Rossum")

### 3) Current stable version number
- **3.14.3**
  - Evidence (infobox): The page shows **“Stable release 3.14.3 / 3 February 2026 (3 February 2026)”**. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language) — quote: "Stable release\n3.14.3\n/ 3 February 2026\n(\n3 February 2026\n)")

(Additional confirmation in article text): The page also says **“As of January 2026, Python 3.14.3 is the latest stable release.”** (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language) — quote: "As of January 2026\n, Python 3.14.3 is the latest stable release.")
final_output.action_summary: From https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Python_(programming_language): First release year (First appeared) = 1991; creator (Designed by) = Guido van Rossum; current stable version = 3.14.3.
